smoke_query_a: SELECT 1 AS c1, 2 AS c2, 3 AS c3
test_path: Downloaded SQLite JDBC jar from Maven Central to /tmp at runtime. Constructed a Java URLClassLoader rooted at the existing thread context loader, loaded `org.sqlite.JDBC` via that loader, instantiated the driver, registered it with java.sql.DriverManager, and set the loader as the thread context CL so Spark's `Utils.classForName` finds the class. Spark JDBC then connected to in-memory SQLite, ran `SELECT 1 AS c1, 2 AS c2, 3 AS c3`, and a 5-row arithmetic query — both successful. Demonstrates that custom JDBC drivers CAN be loaded at runtime in AIDP without a cluster restart, when the JAR is reachable (Maven Central works; PyPI does not).
fix_origin: v0.3.0 — runtime-load helper added to `oracle_ai_data_platform_connectors.jdbc.runtime_load.add_jdbc_jar_at_runtime()` and skill SKILL.md updated to document this as Option A (no cluster restart required); cluster Library tab demoted to Option B.
